name: Lew Tabackin
abstract: Lewis Barry Tabackin (born March 26, 1940) is an American jazz tenor saxophonist and flutist. He is married to pianist Toshiko Akiyoshi with whom he has co-led large ensembles since the 1970s.

about_quotes.speaker: Toshiko Akiyoshi
about_quotes.evidence: because of Lew’s job with the Tonight Show, he went to Los Angeles and out from boredom he remembered that I had four charts, so he suggested he would get the musicians together and we rehearsed every Wednesday morning... So that’s how it started.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toshiko Akiyoshi interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Akiyoshi Toshiko
about_quotes.evidence: that was Lew, and that‟s the first time I met him, and then I said, “I want to cop him for my concert.” ... So that was the first time, actually this was the first time we played together, quartet.
about_quotes.doc_title: Akiyoshi Toshiko — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Goodwin
about_quotes.evidence: Early 80’s I guess, was Phil Woods and Lew Tabackin. And the rhythm section was me and Michael Moore on bass and Jimmy Rowles on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Goodwin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, May 28, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: Probably the most recent group I heard, well, this past year, six months ago, was the Toshiko Akiyoshi - Lew Tabackin band.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Toshiko and Lew Tabackin. [INTERVIEWEE] They came back? I don't know what's wrong over there.

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: Lew [Tabackin] is . . . Lew – Lew great, but Joe for the depth of ideas. As was Lew, as is Hubert.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
